summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a solid May ’77 show, highlighted by an epic Sugaree and stellar Garcia solos.
review: The Miller‑remastered SBD delivers crystal‑clear audio that lets the band’s nuances shine, especially Jerry Garcia’s magical phrasing on Peggy‑O and the extended, soaring Sugaree. While most of the set glows – with Looks Like Rain, Music Never Stopped and a strong Scarlet Begonias/Fire on the Mountain pairing – a few numbers (Promised Land, Tennessee Jed) feel sluggish and the second‑set’s heart is noted as more laid‑back than later ’77 peaks.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 5 stars praising both performance and audio fidelity; only one moderate 3‑star review notes minor setlist issues, leading to a strong overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix praised as the best source; occasional tape cuts are patched but do not detract from overall fidelity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set runs long and includes a sluggish start but picks up with Sugaree and Looks Like Rain; second set opens strong, peaks at Scarlet/Fire, then tapers off after Fire on the Mountain.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s phrasing on Peggy‑O is called magical and his solo work on Sugaree is described as epic.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass receives praise for perfect “boat launch” waves during Good Lovin' and overall depth.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob (referred to as Bobby) is noted as more active than usual, adding solid rhythm work.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux attempts a Mexicali feel but falls into perfunctory runs on Mama Tried/El Paso; generally adequate keyboard work.
